question_text: A nuclear power station operates at 33% efficiency while a modern gas power station operates at 50% efficiency. If both produce the same electrical power output, which statement is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: The nuclear power station must burn more fuel to produce the same output.

options.label: B
options.text: The gas power station wastes more total energy than the nuclear station.

options.label: C
options.text: The nuclear power station is more efficient and produces less waste.

options.label: D
options.text: Both power stations waste exactly the same amount of energy.

correct_answer: A) The nuclear power station must burn more fuel to produce the same output.
explanation: At lower efficiency (33%), the nuclear station must use more input energy to produce the same electrical output compared to the gas station (50% efficiency). This means if they produce equal electrical power, the nuclear station requires more fuel or fissile material as input.
